stories.title: A Day Out with Little Rabbit
sentences: Little Rabbit is very happy today.
He wants to buy a new comic book at the bookstore.
The bookstore is next to the science museum.
Little Rabbit is in front of the post office now.
He asks a friendly turtle, 'How can I get to the bookstore?'
The turtle says, 'Go straight and you can see a hospital.'
Turn left at the hospital and walk for three minutes.
The bookstore is behind the cinema.
Little Rabbit follows the directions carefully.
He sees the cinema and turns right.
Oh no, he is at the museum now, not the bookstore!
Then he sees the bookstore right next to the museum.
He finally finds his favorite book and feels very glad.

questions.question: The bookstore is next to the science museum.
questions.explanation: The third sentence clearly states the bookstore is next to the science museum.

questions.question: Where is Little Rabbit at the beginning of the story?
options: In front of the post office
questions.answer: In front of the post office
questions.explanation: The story mentions Little Rabbit is in front of the post office when he starts asking for directions.

questions.question: Little Rabbit should turn left at the ________.
questions.explanation: The turtle told him to turn left at the hospital.

stories.title: Wu Binbin's Robot Class
sentences: Wu Binbin has a robot class at the science museum today.
He is at the bus stop, but he is lost.
He sees a police officer and asks, 'Where is the science museum?'
The officer says, 'It is near the school.'
Wu Binbin asks, 'Is there a cinema near here?'
The officer smiles and says, 'Yes, there is.'
Go straight from here to the first crossing.
Turn right at the crossing and walk to the cinema.
The science museum is behind the cinema.
Wu Binbin says, 'Thank you very much!'
He walks fast and reaches the crossing quickly.
He finds the cinema and sees the museum behind it.
Wu Binbin is on time for his robot class.

questions.question: Wu Binbin turns left at the crossing to find the cinema.
questions.explanation: The story states he must turn right at the crossing.

questions.question: What is the science museum near according to the officer?
questions.explanation: The officer tells Wu Binbin that the museum is near the school.

questions.question: The science museum is ________ the cinema.
questions.explanation: According to the story, the science museum is located behind the cinema.

stories.title: A Green Way to Travel
sentences: Sarah lives near her school, so she usually goes to school on foot.
Today, she meets her friend Mike at the street corner.
Mike often comes to school by bike because his home is far.
They see a yellow light and wait together.
Sarah says we must stop and wait at a red light.
It is very important to pay attention to the traffic lights.
When the light turns green, they walk across the road carefully.
The school is next to a large park.
Some students come by bus, and some come by subway.
Robin always comes to school by school bus at 7:30 a.m.
Sarah thinks walking is good exercise and very green.
They arrive at the classroom early and start their lessons.

questions.question: Sarah usually goes to school by bus.
questions.explanation: The story mentions that Sarah lives near her school, so she usually goes to school on foot.

questions.question: What must they do when they see a red light?
questions.explanation: The text states 'we must stop and wait at a red light'.

questions.question: Mike often comes to school by ____ because his home is far.
questions.explanation: According to the story, Mike often comes to school by bike.

stories.title: The Big Trip to the Park
sentences: Mr. Black wants to take the class to the Nature Park today.
Most students usually come to school by bus or by subway.
Zhang Peng says the park is far from the school.
How can we get there?
Amy suggests going by bus because it is very convenient.
The teacher reminds everyone to be safe on the road.
Don't run when you cross the road.
We should look at the traffic lights first.
Sometimes students go to the museum by plane for a long trip.
But for the park, taking the bus is the best way.
They wait at the bus stop and get on the bus one by one.
Everyone is happy and enjoys the beautiful view outside.

questions.question: The students are going to the Nature Park.
questions.explanation: The first sentence says Mr. Black wants to take the class to the Nature Park.

questions.question: Why does Amy suggest going by bus?
questions.explanation: The story says Amy suggests going by bus because it is very convenient.

questions.question: Where do the students wait for the bus?
questions.explanation: The story says 'They wait at the bus stop and get on the bus'.

stories.title: A Special Film Plan
sentences: It is Friday today.
Zhang Peng is going to have a busy weekend.
On Saturday morning, he is going to visit his grandparents.
In the afternoon, he is going to the bookstore to buy a new comic book.
He is very excited about Sunday.
He is going to see a film about robots with his friend Sarah.
They are going to meet in front of the cinema at 2:00 p.m.
After the film, which they will enjoy, they are going to have pizza.
Sarah is going to bring her new camera to take some photos.
Zhang Peng wants to learn how to make robots in the future.
This weekend plan makes him very happy.
What are you going to do this weekend?

questions.question: Zhang Peng is going to visit his grandparents on Saturday morning.
questions.explanation: The story states 'On Saturday morning, he is going to visit his grandparents.'

questions.question: What is Zhang Peng going to buy at the bookstore?
questions.explanation: The story mentions he is going to the bookstore to buy a new comic book.

questions.question: Zhang Peng and Sarah are going to see a film about ____.
questions.explanation: The text says 'He is going to see a film about robots'.

stories.title: The Super Trip
sentences: Next week is a long holiday.
Amy and her family are going to take a trip.
They are going to the countryside by bus.
Amy's father is going to go fishing there.
Amy's mother is going to draw some beautiful pictures of flowers.
What is Amy going to do?
She is going to pick apples with her cousins.
They are going to have a big dinner on Sunday evening.
Amy needs to buy a new pair of shoes for the trip.
She is going to the shoe store tomorrow morning.
She is going to get up early at 7:00 a.m.
They are all going to have a wonderful time together.

questions.question: Amy's family is going to the countryside by train.
questions.explanation: The story says they are going to the countryside by bus, not train.

questions.question: When is Amy going to the shoe store?
questions.explanation: The text states 'She is going to the shoe store tomorrow morning.'

questions.question: Amy is going to ___ with her cousins.
questions.explanation: The story says 'She is going to pick apples with her cousins.'

stories.title: Wu Binbin's New Pen Pal
sentences: Wu Binbin has a new pen pal from Australia.
His name is Peter and he lives on a farm.
Peter is very tall and he likes playing football.
He also likes reading stories about robots in the evening.
Does he live in Sydney?
No, he lives in a small village with his grandparents.
Peter's father is a fisherman and he goes to sea.
Peter studies hard and he also likes doing kung fu.
Wu Binbin wants to visit Peter during the summer holiday.
He is writing an email to Peter right now.
He tells Peter about his own hobby, singing.
They both like making robots and sharing their ideas.

questions.question: Peter lives in a big city in Australia.
questions.explanation: The story says Peter lives on a farm in a small village, not a big city.

questions.question: What are Peter's hobbies according to the story?
options: Playing football and doing kung fu
questions.answer: Playing football and doing kung fu
questions.explanation: The text mentions Peter likes playing football, reading stories, and doing kung fu.

questions.question: Wu Binbin tells Peter about his own hobby, which is ________.
questions.explanation: The sentence says: 'He tells Peter about his own hobby, singing.'

stories.title: A Letter from London
sentences: Sarah has a pen pal named Alice.
Alice lives in London and she goes to school by bus.
What are Alice's hobbies?
She likes dancing and she often goes to the gym.
She also likes climbing mountains with her brother on weekends.
Alice's mother is a doctor and she works in a hospital.
Her father is a cook and he likes making delicious food.
Does Alice like singing?
Yes, she likes singing English songs very much.
Sarah is happy because she has many things in common with Alice.
Sarah likes dancing and singing too.
They want to be friends for a long time.

questions.question: Alice's father is a cook.
questions.explanation: The story states: 'Her father is a cook and he likes making delicious food.'

questions.question: How does Alice go to school?
questions.explanation: The text says 'she goes to school by bus.'

questions.question: On weekends, Alice likes ________ mountains with her brother.
questions.explanation: The story mentions she likes climbing mountains on weekends.

questions.question: What hobbies do Sarah and Alice share?
questions.explanation: The text says Sarah likes dancing and singing too, matching Alice's interests.

stories.title: A Day with Uncle Li
sentences: My Uncle Li has a very exciting life.
What does he do?
He is a brave pilot.
He works for a big airline company.
He likes flying planes in the sky.
He goes to work by taxi every morning.
Sometimes he flies to different countries like Japan and Australia.
Uncle Li stays healthy because he likes sports.
If you like sports, you can be a pilot or a coach.
My father is different from Uncle Li.
He is a scientist and works in a university.
He often goes to work by subway because it is fast.

questions.question: Uncle Li is a scientist and works in a university.
questions.explanation: The story says Uncle Li is a pilot; the father is the scientist.

questions.question: How does Uncle Li get to his workplace?
questions.explanation: The story mentions he goes to work by taxi every morning.

questions.question: If you like sports, the story suggests you can be a pilot or a ______.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'If you like sports, you can be a pilot or a coach.'

stories.title: Sarah's Dream Job
sentences: Today is Career Day at Sarah's school.
Sarah wants to talk about her aunt, Ms. Chen.
What does she do?
Ms. Chen is a famous headteacher.
She works in a primary school.
She likes helping children and reading books.
Ms. Chen lives very near the school.
She usually goes to work on foot.
Sarah's brother, Tom, likes drawing pictures very much.
If you like drawing, you can be an artist.
Tom wants to work in an art gym or a factory.
Sarah wants to be a reporter because she likes talking to people.

questions.question: Ms. Chen usually goes to work on foot.
questions.explanation: The story says she lives near the school and goes to work on foot.

questions.question: Why does Sarah want to be a reporter?
options: Because she likes talking to people
questions.answer: Because she likes talking to people
questions.explanation: The last sentence says Sarah wants to be a reporter because she likes talking to people.

questions.question: Where does Ms. Chen work?
questions.explanation: The text mentions she works in a primary school.

stories.title: Robin's Bad Morning
sentences: Today is Monday, but Robin looks very sad in the classroom.
He can't find his new red pen anywhere.
Robin is worried because he has a big English test today.
His friend Sarah sees him and asks, 'How do you feel, Robin?'
Robin says, 'I feel afraid that I can't write my name.'
Sarah says, 'Don't be sad, you can use my blue pen.'
Then Sarah tells him, 'You should take a deep breath.'
Robin does what Sarah says and feels much better now.
If you feel worried, what should you do?
You should talk to your friends or teachers for help.
Robin is happy and ready for the test at last.

questions.question: Robin is sad because he lost his new red pen.
questions.explanation: The story mentions Robin can't find his new red pen anywhere.

questions.question: What does Sarah suggest Robin to do to feel better?
questions.explanation: Sarah specifically says 'You should take a deep breath' in the story.

questions.question: If you feel worried, you should talk to your friends or ______.
questions.explanation: The story suggests talking to friends or teachers when feeling worried.

stories.title: The Angry Little Brother
sentences: Sam is playing a computer game, but his little brother breaks it.
Now Sam is very angry and his face is red.
His mother comes into the room and looks at him.
She says, 'Sam, you shouldn't be so angry at your brother.'
Sam asks, 'If I feel angry, what should I do?'
His mother says, 'First, you should sit down and count to ten.'
Sam sits on the chair and counts slowly from one to ten.
He also thinks about his favorite cartoon and feels a little happy.
His little brother says sorry and gives him a big hug.
Sam is not angry anymore and they go outside to play.
It is important to stay cool when we are mad.
vocabulary_used: If I feel angry, what should I do?

questions.question: Why is Sam very angry at the beginning?
options: He wants to see a doctor
questions.explanation: The story says Sam's little brother breaks his computer game.

questions.question: Sam's mother tells him to see a doctor.
questions.explanation: His mother tells him to sit down and count to ten, not see a doctor.

questions.question: Sam counts slowly from one to ______ to feel better.
questions.explanation: The story says Sam counts slowly from one to ten.

stories.title: A Busy Weekend Plan
sentences: Robin is writing his weekend plan on a piece of paper.
On Saturday morning, he is going to the post office by bike.
He wants to send a postcard to his pen pal in Australia.
His pen pal likes collecting stamps and going hiking in the mountains.
In the afternoon, Robin will go to the cinema to see a film.
The cinema is next to the bookstore on the left side of North Street.
He needs to take the No. 5 bus and get off at the hospital.
On Sunday, Robin is going to help his mother wash the clothes.
He is a hard-working boy and his parents are very proud of him.
If he feels tired, he should take a deep breath and count to ten.
Robin is happy because he has a very busy and meaningful weekend.
He believes that doing hobbies makes life more colorful and fun.

questions.question: Robin's pen pal lives in Australia and likes collecting stamps.
questions.explanation: The story mentions the pen pal is in Australia and likes collecting stamps and going hiking.

questions.question: How does Robin get to the cinema?
questions.explanation: The text states he needs to take the No. 5 bus and get off at the hospital.

questions.question: If Robin feels tired, he should take a deep breath and ______.
questions.explanation: The story suggests he should count to ten to help manage his feelings.

stories.title: The Lion and the Clever Mouse
sentences: Once upon a time, a big lion was sleeping in the forest.
A small mouse ran over the lion’s face and woke him up.
The lion was very angry and wanted to eat the little mouse.
'Please don't eat me!' the mouse cried out in fear.
'I will help you someday if you let me go now.'
The lion laughed but he let the mouse go anyway.
The next week, some hunters caught the lion with a big net.
The lion was sad and worried because he could not get out.
Suddenly, the mouse came and used his teeth to bite the net.
Finally, the lion was free and he thanked the little mouse.
The lion learned that even a small friend can be a great help.
We should be kind to others and never look down on anyone.

questions.question: What is the 'setting' of this story?
questions.explanation: The story begins by stating the lion was sleeping in the forest.

questions.question: The hunters caught the lion with a ______.
questions.explanation: The text says hunters caught the lion with a big net.

questions.question: The lion stayed angry at the mouse until the end of the story.
questions.explanation: The lion thanked the mouse and learned a lesson about being kind.

questions.question: What is the 'moral' or main idea of this fable?
options: Lions are the kings of the forest.
Even a small friend can be a great help.
Sleeping in the forest is dangerous.
questions.answer: Even a small friend can be a great help.
questions.explanation: The story explicitly states this lesson at the end after the mouse saves the lion.
